_meta.purpose: Entity discovery for known AND unknown assets, regardless of ingest pathway (decisions #17/#18). A runtime field performs multi-entity EXTRACTION per document -- existence attestation, not single attribution -- emitting every entity the document proves exists; the terms group_by fans each document into every matching entity bucket. Slots: SELF (host.name, host.hostname, agent.name, deduplicated), OBSERVER (observer.hostname else observed-<observer.ip>), DEVICE (log.syslog.hostname else observed-<log.source.address ip, port stripped> -- only collector-stamped fields, so ordinary network events never mint entities from conversation participants), and an empty-document fallback (observed-<host.ip or source.ip>) reachable only when no slot yielded anything. A collector-mediated syslog document therefore attests the collector, any observer, and the downstream device -- up to three entities from one document. Output docs carry host.name + @timestamp, eligible as an entity-store feed: add m_26_14-observed to the Security default data view.
_meta.signals: IP-keyed (observed-<ip>) entities are observed-but-unexpected by definition (quadrant model). Relay/NAT collapse signature: one IP-keyed entity with many distinct appnames/datasets. DHCP churn mints a new entity per lease; remediate via entity resolution.
_meta.troubleshooting: If m_26_14-observed is empty or stale: GET _transform/m_26_14-observed-assets/_stats. Documents yielding no ladder identity (no name, no usable IP) are skipped by design. Source index patterns are edited here until they become a package policy variable at M8.
_meta.semantics: event_count and all aggregations mean 'events REFERENCING this entity', not 'events produced by it' -- a collector's counts include traffic it relayed. Relay-collapse signature (one IP-keyed entity, many appnames) persists only when a relay rewrites source addresses AND devices send no syslog header hostnames.
_meta.sync_caveat: Continuous sync watermarks on @timestamp: documents arriving with event timestamps older than the last checkpoint's upper bound (minus the 120s delay) are NEVER processed -- silently. This affects backfilled/late-arriving data (syslog replays, air-gapped transfers) and any test data written with backdated timestamps. T&E: load data BEFORE starting the transform, or _stop + _reset + _start to re-run discovery over everything. Production mitigation options (M8 decision): sync on event.ingested (monotonic, but absent on classic Beats paths -- docs missing the sync field are skipped entirely), periodic reset, or scheduled batch recompute.
_meta.timestamps: @timestamp on dest docs = ledger write time (stamped by the m_26_14-observed-freshness dest pipeline, required for entity store v2 lookback extraction); last_log = last source activity. The scoring collector and staleness logic read last_log.
_meta.identity: IP-keyed IDs from the observer/device slots are namespaced by collection point ('observed-<ip>@<collector>'): reused private IP space stays distinct per L3 domain, and the suffix is a parent pointer for triage. The collector suffix prefers observer.name (stable site/pool id set at ingest, e.g. a Logstash VIP or plant code) over host.name (per-node identity) so load-balanced Logstash pools do not mint duplicate entities per backend. Plain 'observed-<ip>' appears only via the empty-document fallback. One device via two collectors = two entities until resolved (visible, operator-fixable) -- preferred over silent wrong-merges.

script.source: Set ids = new HashSet(); String selfName = null; if (doc.containsKey('host.name') && doc['host.name'].size() > 0) { selfName = doc['host.name'].value.toLowerCase(); ids.add(selfName); } if (doc.containsKey('host.hostname') && doc['host.hostname'].size() > 0) { ids.add(doc['host.hostname'].value.toLowerCase()); } if (doc.containsKey('agent.name') && doc['agent.name'].size() > 0) { ids.add(doc['agent.name'].value.toLowerCase()); } String collectorKey = null; if (doc.containsKey('observer.name') && doc['observer.name'].size() > 0) { collectorKey = doc['observer.name'].value.toLowerCase(); } else if (selfName != null) { collectorKey = selfName; } String sfx = collectorKey != null ? '@' + collectorKey : ''; boolean obsNamed = false; if (doc.containsKey('observer.hostname') && doc['observer.hostname'].size() > 0) { ids.add(doc['observer.hostname'].value.toLowerCase()); obsNamed = true; } if (!obsNamed && doc.containsKey('observer.ip') && doc['observer.ip'].size() > 0) { ids.add('observed-' + doc['observer.ip'].value.toString() + sfx); } String dev = null; if (doc.containsKey('log.syslog.hostname') && doc['log.syslog.hostname'].size() > 0) { String s = doc['log.syslog.hostname'].value.trim().toLowerCase(); if (s.endsWith('.')) { s = s.substring(0, s.length() - 1); } if (s != '' && s != '-') { dev = s; } } if (dev == null && doc.containsKey('log.source.address') && doc['log.source.address'].size() > 0) { String a = doc['log.source.address'].value.trim(); String ip = null; if (a.startsWith('[')) { int e = a.indexOf(']'); if (e > 0) { ip = a.substring(1, e); } } else { int f = a.indexOf(':'); int l = a.lastIndexOf(':'); if (f == -1) { ip = a; } else if (f == l) { ip = a.substring(0, f); } else { ip = a; } } if (ip != null && ip != '') { dev = 'observed-' + ip + sfx; } } if (dev != null) { ids.add(dev); } if (ids.isEmpty()) { if (doc.containsKey('host.ip') && doc['host.ip'].size() > 0) { ids.add('observed-' + doc['host.ip'].value.toString()); } else if (doc.containsKey('source.ip') && doc['source.ip'].size() > 0) { ids.add('observed-' + doc['source.ip'].value.toString()); } } for (def id : ids) { String v = id.toString().trim(); if (v != '' && v != 'observed-') { emit(v); } }
